summary: API routes implementation is well-structured with comprehensive endpoint coverage across 9 route modules. Strong validation and error handling patterns are present. However, critical issues prevent production readiness: runtime validation failed due to missing dependency installation, most endpoints contain placeholder implementations (TODO comments), and several security/performance requirements need implementation.

runtime_validation.import_test: FAIL - ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'sqlalchemy' (dependencies not installed)
runtime_validation.app_startup: FAIL - Cannot test without dependencies installed
runtime_validation.reserved_names: PASS - No Pydantic/SQLAlchemy reserved name conflicts detected
runtime_validation.async_patterns: PASS - All route handlers use async/await correctly, no sync-blocking calls
runtime_validation.type_hints: NOT_TESTED - Requires mypy and installed dependencies

details.notes: Comprehensive endpoint coverage across all required modules. All 9 route files properly registered in main.py with /api/v1 prefix.
Strong Pydantic validation across all endpoints. Request/response models properly defined with Field descriptions and constraints.
Auth middleware properly implemented with get_current_user, get_current_admin dependencies. Projects DELETE requires admin, logout requires auth, public endpoints clearly marked.
Consistent error format with error_code, message, details structure. Appropriate HTTP status codes used. Missing trace/correlation IDs for request tracking.
All routes have docstrings and FastAPI descriptions. Summary and description parameters used. Missing request/response examples for OpenAPI docs.
Comprehensive RateLimitMiddleware implemented with sliding window. Returns standard X-RateLimit-* headers. Configurable per-endpoint limits. Uses in-memory store (should be Redis for production).

SQLAlchemy parameterized queries prevent SQL injection. Pydantic validation sanitizes input. No obvious XSS vectors. However, upload endpoints need additional file content validation (magic number checks).

Pagination properly implemented with PaginationParams (max 100 items). Projects list uses filters and pagination. Cannot verify N+1 queries without database access. Missing request timeouts and query timeouts configuration.

issues.issue: Dependencies not installed - app cannot start
issues.description: Runtime validation failed because required dependencies (sqlalchemy, fastapi, etc.) are not installed. Cannot verify app startup or imports.
issues.recommendation: Run 'pip install -r backend/requirements.txt' in appropriate virtual environment

issues.description: Most endpoints contain TODO comments indicating incomplete implementation. Database queries, service calls, and business logic are placeholders returning mock data.
issues.recommendation: Implement service layer methods, database queries, and integrate with Anthropic API, GCS, and Google Sheets

issues.description: No timeout configuration for HTTP requests, database queries, or external API calls. Could lead to hanging requests and resource exhaustion.
issues.recommendation: Add timeouts to uvicorn config, database session config, httpx client, and Anthropic API calls

issues.issue: Missing trace/correlation IDs in error responses
issues.description: Error responses don't include trace IDs for request tracking and debugging. Makes troubleshooting production issues difficult.
issues.recommendation: Add middleware to generate correlation IDs and include in all responses and logs

issues.issue: File upload security - missing magic number validation
issues.description: Upload endpoints only validate content-type headers which can be spoofed. Missing file content validation (magic numbers) to verify actual file type.
issues.recommendation: Add magic number checking using python-magic or similar to verify file signatures match claimed content-type

issues.issue: In-memory rate limiting not suitable for production
issues.description: RateLimitStore uses in-memory dict which won't work with multiple workers/instances. Acknowledged in comments but not yet implemented.
issues.recommendation: Implement Redis-backed rate limiting for production deployment with multiple workers

issues.description: While descriptions are present, OpenAPI docs would benefit from concrete examples for request/response bodies.
issues.recommendation: Add example parameter to response_model decorators and Pydantic Field definitions

issues.description: DELETE /jobs/{id} endpoint exists but has TODO comment and doesn't actually delete jobs.
issues.recommendation: Implement job_repository.delete_job() method or remove endpoint if not needed

positive_findings: Excellent code organization with clear separation between routes, services, models, and config
Consistent error handling pattern with structured error responses (error_code, message, details)
Comprehensive authentication system with JWT, refresh tokens, OAuth state validation, and token rotation
Strong input validation using Pydantic models with Field constraints and descriptions
Proper use of async/await throughout - no sync-blocking calls detected
Rate limiting middleware with sliding window algorithm and standard headers
Streaming file uploads to avoid memory issues with large files
Proper CORS and security middleware configuration
Admin role checking for protected operations (delete, overrides)
Pagination enforced on list endpoints with reasonable defaults (max 100 items)
Comprehensive logging with structured log messages
Clean dependency injection pattern using FastAPI Depends
Version-prefixed API routes (/api/v1) for future compatibility
HTTP-only cookie support for refresh tokens
Proper HTTP status codes (201 for creates, 204 for deletes, 422 for validation, 429 for rate limits)
Health check endpoints for monitoring and load balancers

recommendations.recommendation: Set up proper dependency management and environment
recommendations.details: Create virtual environment, install requirements.txt, configure environment variables from .env file
Implement all TODO items in service layer: database queries, Anthropic API integration, GCS uploads, Google Sheets integration
Configure timeouts at multiple levels: uvicorn server (--timeout-keep-alive), database sessions (pool_timeout, query_timeout), httpx client (timeout=30), Anthropic API (timeout=60)
Add correlation ID middleware, include trace IDs in all log statements and error responses, consider OpenTelemetry integration
Implement magic number checking for uploaded files, add virus scanning for production, validate PDF structure
Replace in-memory RateLimitStore with Redis for distributed rate limiting across multiple workers/instances
Add example request/response bodies to improve API documentation and developer experience
Create integration tests for all endpoints, mock external services (Anthropic, GCS), use pytest fixtures for database

test_coverage_notes.static_analysis: Completed - no syntax errors, proper async patterns, no reserved name conflicts
test_coverage_notes.recommendation: Install dependencies and re-run validation to complete runtime checks

next_steps: 1. Install backend dependencies: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt
3. Run database migrations: alembic upgrade head
4. Complete service layer implementations (remove TODO comments)
5. Add timeout configurations to all external calls
7. Add file magic number validation to upload endpoints
8. Run full test suite: pytest backend/tests
9. Re-run QA-API-001 validation after implementations complete
10. Deploy to staging environment for integration testing

pass_criteria.status: FAIL - Score below 85% and critical/high issues present
